stemHtml: You are departing a desert area early in the morning, planning a climb to 8000 ft. The rate of climb calculated from the AFM is 1500 ft/min, but passing 6000 ft you notice a decreased rate of climb of 1260 ft/min. What is the most likely reason?

options.a: the air pressure increases as you climb
options.b: the air temperature is rapidly decreasing due to an inversion layer
options.c: temperature increases with altitude due to an inversion layer
options.d: the pressure altitude decreases as you climb
